sections.paragraph:
(shortened from אָבֵן, from the root כּוּן; comp. Ch. הָכֵין, הָכֵי and בְּ, which is taken from the fuller כֵּן: [Derivation given in Ges. corr.: “kindred are כִּי, כֵּן, אָכֵן, see Hupfeld in Zeitschr. f. d. Morgenl. ii. 143.”]).-

(1) affirmative part. surely, certainly, no doubt. Gen 44:28, אַךְ טָרֹף טֹרַף “no doubt he is torn;” Jud. 3:24 1Ki 22:32 2Ki 24:3 Ps. 58:12 Job 18:21. Hence-

(2) adv. of limitation, only. Exo 10:17, אַךְ הַפַּעַם “only this once;” Lev 11:21, אַךְ אֶת־זֶה תֹּאכְלוּ “only these ye may eat;” Psa 37:8, “be not angry, (for this is) only for doing evil,” i.e. anger is often the cause of crime; Pro 14:23, “vain words אַךְ לְמַחְסֹור only (lead) to want;” Pro 11:24 21:5 . Specially it is used-(a) before adjectives, where only is i.q. quite, altogether. Deu 16:15, וְהָיִיתָ אַךְ שָׂמֵחַ “and thou shalt be only joyful,” i.e. altogether joyful. Isa 16:7, אַךְ נְכָאִים “altogether contrite;” Isa 19:11.-(b) before substantives, nothing but, which may often be rendered solus, merus, Germ. lauter; Engl. merely. Psa 139:11, אַךְ חשֶׁךְ “nothing but darkness,” i.e. merely darkness. Psa 39:12, אַךְ הֶבֶל “nothing but vanity,” i.e. mere vanity.-(c) before adverbs and verbs, quite, altogether. Psa 73:13, אַךְ רִיק “altogether in vain.” 1Sa 25:21 Job 19:13, יֹדְעַי אַךְ זָרוּ מִמֶּנִּי “my acquaintances are quite alienated from me.” Jud. 20:39 Job 23:6. Comp. Exo 12:15, אַךְ בַּיֹּום הָרִאשֹׁון “altogether in the first day,” is, on the first day itself, nur gleich am erften Tage.

(3) an adv. of exception, only, but. Gen 20:12, אַךְ לֹא בַת אִמּי “but not (μονονουχὶ) the daughter of my mother.” Lev 11:4 Num 26:55 Deu 18:20 Jos 3:4.

(4) an adverb of time, only now, for just now, scarcely. Gen 27:30, אַךְ יָצֹא יָצָא יַעֲקֹב … וְעֵשָׂו אָחִיו בָּא “Jacob was but scarcely gone out.… when Esau his brother came in.” Jud. 7:19 . Comp. Cic. ad Fam. viii. 23, “tantum quod ex Arpinati veneram, cum mihi a te litterœ redditœ sint;” Vellej. ii. 117.
